title: Online computer science degree—masters in computer science without cs undergrad
text:
Online computer science degree—masters in computer science without cs undergrad
Align MS in Computer Science Online
Apply now
Request info
The Align MS in Computer Science Online offers a flexible online format with three in-person residences to prepare you to break into tech, without a computer science background. Study with a supportive cohort of your peers as you spend the first two semesters building a solid base of fundamental CS knowledge before diving into emerging areas such as AI, human-computer interaction, game design, security, and more.
Explore program requirements in the course catalog
Program highlights
Semester hours
44 total semester hours required
Start term
Fall admission
Program modality
Online; three in-person residencies
Career prospects and top employers
The Align program prepares people from diverse backgrounds with the computer science foundation and real-world experience needed for success in the field — making our graduates highly sought after by top employers. You’ll also have the chance to build industry connections through an optional paid co-op or internship. Our graduates are employed in positions that include:
Software development engineer at Amazon
Software engineer at AWS
Software engineer at Meta
Product manager at Oracle
Software engineer at Snowflake
IOS developer at Intrepid
Full-stack engineer at Fidelity
Mobile app developer at Bose
70% of our graduates
chose software developer roles for their first job post-graduation.
Within 3 years, 35%
of these alums were promoted to
senior positions
.
Data from 1,000 alumni LinkedIn profiles, 2022
What makes Align different
Learn about the successful collaboration between Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Align students, and what makes it different: “We’re not just bringing in trained engineers. We’re bringing a set of folks who have a different set of perspectives, a different set of experiences. They bring a fresh understanding — a fresh set of eyes,” says Wayne Duso, former AWS vice president.
How the Align program works
Flexibility and connections that work for you
It takes both skills and connections to land a top job in tech. That’s why our online program includes three short in-person residencies — with travel and room and board included in tuition — at the beginning, middle, and end of the online bridge portion of the program. During these residencies, which are hosted at three of our campuses, you’ll have opportunities to build community, get in-person support, and put what you’ve learned into practice with real-world projects in a campus setting.
From there, you can shape the format and pace of your online program in a way that fits into your work and family life: Transition to in-person learning, full or part time, at one of our eight campuses offering the Align program, or continue the online program.
Did you know?
The median salary for software developers in the US is $138,110
US Bureau of Labor Statistics, May 2023
Learning outcomes
The Align MS in Computer Science Online will equip you with technical knowledge and career preparation. As a graduate, you will:
Exhibit proficiency in the design, implementation, and testing
of software
Demonstrate skills and experience
working in small teams
Apply algorithmic and theoretical computer-science principles
to solve computing problems from a variety of application areas
Demonstrate the ability to learn and develop competencies
in specialized or emerging computer science fields
From military pilot to CS professional
Benji Northrop was a military pilot looking for more work-life balance when he joined the Align program. Now, he says, its model of fully asynchronous classes and “commitment from the school from start to finish” allow him to spend more time with his family while feeling fully supported in his program.
Meet our faculty
Get to know the engaged, experienced professors who are here to support you every step of the way.
View all faculty
Albert Lionelle
Albert Lionelle is an associate teaching professor at Khoury College, and the director of the Align Online program. His research centers on the use of computing tools and inclusive pedagogy to enable better computer science education.
Read bio
Applications and admissions
Deadline: August 1 for fall admission
How to apply
How to apply
Our Align Apply page has everything you need to know about applying to the Align MS in Computer Science program at Khoury College, including:
Align admissions requirements
Timelines and deadlines
Resources and contact information
Learn about applying to Align
Degree costs
Degree costs
Visit Student Financial Services for current tuition and fee information, and to find tools and calculators to help you estimate the cost of your degree, based on 44 required semester hours.
Visit Student Financial Services
Financial support
Financial support
Pursuing a master’s degree is a significant investment in your future. Northeastern University and Khoury College of Computer Sciences are here to help support you in any way we can. From financial aid to scholarship opportunities to co-op placements and teaching assistantships, there are multiple options to help fund your degree as you work toward your goals.
Learn more about financial support
